notes: Roman political figure, member of the Second Triumvirate with Antony and Octavian, known for his unstable and fickle character.

docs.title: Letter I
docs.author: Marcus Tullius Cicero
docs.excerpt: … whom the former was the more fickle of the two, the latter the more corrupt, but both of whom dreaded peace and were enemies to quiet. Against these men, inflamed with the ambition of revolutionizing the state, we had no protecting force to oppose.

docs.title: Annals, Book 3
docs.author: Tacitus
docs.excerpt: … against him, for they thought Lepidus gentle rather than cowardly, and that his inherited poverty, with the high rank in which he had lived without a blot, ought to be considered a credit to instead of a reproach.
